value.basis: Saturday report date; JPX officially closes Saturdays and Sundays; HKEX was already closed Friday June 19 and reopens Monday June 22; live market-data sources show latest covered index observations on June 18 or June 19, not June 20

market_session: Asia-Pacific weekend / no regular cash-equity session on report date
text: There was no fresh Asia-Pacific cash-equity session to report for Saturday, June 20, 2026. The segment therefore carries forward the latest verified regional closes: Japan, India, Korea, Australia and Singapore from Friday, June 19; Hong Kong, mainland China and Taiwan from Thursday, June 18 because Hong Kong and mainland China were closed Friday for the Dragon Boat Festival holiday and the Taiwan source's latest listed observation is also June 18.

nikkei_225.basis: FRED close corroborated by Trading Economics JP225

market_session: latest verified regional closes carried into the June 20 weekend report date

calculation.method: Use source-reported close, point change and percent change; Nikkei point change cross-check: 71,250.06 minus 71,053.49 equals 196.57, or about 0.28%. Other non-FRED benchmarks are vendor-reported CFD-tracked market references and are not restated as official exchange settlements.

text: The latest verified Asia-Pacific snapshot was mixed and date-staggered: Nikkei 225 71,250.06 (+0.28%) on June 19; HK50 23,924.81 (-1.59%) and Shanghai Composite 4,090.48 (-0.43%) on June 18 before the Dragon Boat holiday; CSI 300 4,941.60 (+0.21%) on June 18; SENSEX 76,802.90 (-0.78%) and NIFTY 50 24,013.10 (-0.64%) on June 19; KOSPI 9,052.42 (-0.13%) on June 19; Taiwan TSI 46,465.20 (+1.28%) on June 18; ASX200 8,828.70 (-0.92%) and Singapore STI 5,192.70 (-0.39%) on June 19. These are carried-forward closes, not June 20 prints.

value.all_time_high_context: Trading Economics says the Japan Stock Market Index reached an all-time high in June 2026

market_session: Tokyo regular close on Friday 2026-06-19, carried into the June 20 weekend

calculation.method: FRED close 71,250.06 minus previous close 71,053.49 equals +196.57 points; +196.57 / 71,053.49 = +0.2766%, rounded to +0.28%.

text: Japan was the clean positive outlier among June 19 Asia closes: the Nikkei 225 closed at 71,250.06, up 196.57 points or 0.28% from June 18. Trading Economics' JP225 page corroborated the 71,250 level and said technology shares mostly advanced while financial, industrial and consumer stocks were generally weaker, with the index near a June 2026 record zone.

hong_kong.friday_status: HKEX markets closed June 19 and re-open June 22

mainland_china.friday_status: Trading Economics states markets would be closed June 19 for Dragon Boat Festival

taiwan.basis: latest listed Taiwan observation in the accessed source

market_session: June 18 latest listed closes carried through the June 19 Dragon Boat holiday and June 20 weekend
text: Greater China was a holiday-carry rather than a live June 20 read. Hong Kong's HK50 was last listed at 23,924.81 on June 18, down 1.59%, and HKEX states Hong Kong markets were closed June 19 and re-open June 22. Mainland China's June 18 read was split, with the Shanghai Composite down 0.43% to 4,090.48 while CSI 300 rose 0.21% to 4,941.60; the same China source states markets would be closed June 19 for Dragon Boat Festival. Taiwan's latest listed source observation was June 18, with TSI up 1.28% to 46,465.20.

value.weekly_context: Trading Economics says the index advanced 1.7% for the week despite Friday's slide

market_session: India regular close on Friday 2026-06-19, carried into the June 20 weekend
text: India ended the last available session lower but not as a week-level breakdown: on June 19, SENSEX fell 607.07 points, or 0.78%, to 76,802.90 and NIFTY 50 fell 154.90 points, or 0.64%, to 24,013.10. Trading Economics still described the index as up 1.7% for the week, with TCS, HDFC Bank, Reliance and Infosys among major laggards while Bharti Airtel and ICICI Bank were positive.

south_korea.context: profit-taking after recent record-high rally; foreign investors remained net buyers

australia.context: non-energy minerals and industrials weaker; BHP hit by Jansen cost-overrun and impairment news; weekly gain still 0.3%

singapore.context: bank-component weakness; index remained near June 2026 all-time-high zone

market_session: Friday 2026-06-19 regional regular closes, carried into the June 20 weekend
text: Korea, Australia and Singapore all carried softer June 19 closes into the weekend: KOSPI slipped 0.13% to 9,052.42 as profit-taking followed a recent rally to record highs, ASX200 fell 0.92% to 8,828.70 with non-energy minerals and industrials weak and BHP pressured by cost-overrun and impairment news, and Singapore STI lost 0.39% to 5,192.70 with bank components softer.

subject.name: date-staggered Asia carry: AI/semi resilience versus risk-off profit-taking and holiday closure

positive_pockets: Taiwan TSI rose 1.28% on latest listed June 18 observation, with Taiwan Semiconductor +1.05%
pressure_points: South Korea profit-taking after recent record highs as Middle East uncertainty returned
Hong Kong financial, tech and retail shares led June 18 retreat
Australia non-energy minerals and industrials lagged, with BHP pressured by Jansen cost-overrun and impairment news
India large-cap IT and financial laggards outweighed pockets of telecom and bank strength
value.calendar_effect: Dragon Boat Festival closures in Hong Kong and mainland China, plus Saturday weekend, meant no June 20 live print

market_session: latest available regional sessions carried into weekend report date
text: The Asia read was not a single synchronized session. The constructive side was concentrated in Japan and Taiwan, where technology/semiconductor exposure supported the latest listed closes. The pressure side was broader but moderate: Korea saw profit-taking as Middle East uncertainty returned, Hong Kong's June 18 losses were led by financial, tech and retail shares, Australia was hit by materials/industrial weakness and BHP-specific news, and India slipped on large-cap IT and financial laggards. The calendar mattered as much as price: Hong Kong and mainland China were closed for Dragon Boat Festival before the Saturday report date.

value.basis: HKEX explicitly says Hong Kong re-opens June 22; FRED Nikkei series lists next release date June 22; JPX official calendar closes Saturdays and Sundays; other covered live index pages had no June 20 regular cash-equity print

market_session: next-session basis after June 20 weekend report date
text: The next actionable Asia cash-equity read begins on Monday, June 22, not on the report date. HKEX explicitly says Hong Kong re-opens on June 22 after the June 19 closure, FRED's Nikkei series lists June 22 as the next release date, and JPX states its markets close on Saturdays and Sundays. Other covered index pages showed latest observations dated June 18 or June 19 rather than June 20.
